analysis_description: Round 3 tapes (days 0–2): Black–Scholes IV per VEV from top-of-book mid vs VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT; quadratic IV smile in m_t=log(K/S)/sqrt(T) with WLS weights 1+bid_vol_1+ask_vol_1 (volume conviction). T from round3description (8/7/6 dte at open for csv days 0/1/2) plus intraday wind dte_eff=dte_open-(timestamp//100)/10000; T_years=dte_eff/365; r=0 (same as round3work/plotting/original_method/combined_analysis/plot_iv_smile_round3.py). Greeks: vega at fitted IV for summary mean. Microstructure: mean top-of-book spread ~3.08 on included strikes.
used_to_decide: Residual IV is roughly mean-zero (mean -4.9e-5); std ~0.012 guides RESID_ENTER scale. Corr(|residual|, log(weight)) ≈ -0.11 supports weighting the smile by book size. Session-open extract mids 5250.0 / 5245.0 / 5267.5 used as backtest csv_day anchors when traderData resets per day.

analysis_description: Extended Greek-aware tape analysis on all timestamps for days 0–2: compute IV via BS inversion from top-of-book mid for each VEV, fit per-timestamp volume-WLS smile residual, then profile |residual| against delta, vega, volume, and spread by strike. Timing remains from round3description: day0/1/2 open DTE 8/7/6 with intraday wind dte_eff = dte_open - (timestamp//100)/10000.
used_to_decide: Residual quality is strike-dependent: VEV_5100..VEV_5500 have tight spreads (about 1.1 to 4.3) and manageable residual magnitudes, while VEV_4000/4500 are much wider (about 15.8 to 20.1) and noisy. This motivated v3/v4 to keep the same residual-fade thesis but restrict active trading to more liquid middle strikes.

analysis_description: Subsampled (timestamp step 500) first differences: VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT mid vs BS implied vol from VEV_5300 mid with K=5300, r=0, T from dte_eff/365. Exploratory link between spot moves and a near-ATM option IV; small negative corr on subsample.
used_to_decide: Documents IV vs spot co-movement on tape (weak negative OLS/corr in this subsample), kept as context for same thesis—no full delta-hedge leg per failed v1. v5 full-chain WLS was abandoned after backtest; v7 keeps closed 5-strike WLS and drops 5000 as trade candidate.

analysis_description: BS call **gamma** at IV inverted from mid (S=VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT mid, r=0, T=dte_eff/365) for VEV_5100..VEV_5500, subsampled every 200 timestamps on days 0–2. Mean gamma peaks near 5200/5300 in this strip (roughly 0.00224) and tapers toward wings; supports vega-scaled sizing and focusing on liquid ATM neighborhood.
used_to_decide: Reinforces that the 5100–5500 strip is the high-curvature region of the smile for the assigned spot range; paired with v8 EMA + book-depth gate to avoid acting on microstructure noise.

analysis_description: BS European **call theta** (per option per year in price space) at IV from mid for VEV_5100..VEV_5500, r=0, T=dte_eff/365, subsampled every 400 timestamps on days 0–2. Mean theta is most negative around 5200/5300 (magnitude ~1850 vs ~750 at 5500 in this subsample), consistent with higher time decay vega concentration near ATM.
used_to_decide: Documents time-decay profile on the traded strip; parameter sweeps v9 (vega-weighted WLS) and v10 (EMA/threshold) both underperformed v8, so we keep v8's linear book weighting in WLS for now.

analysis_description: Lag-1 autocorrelation of raw IV smile residuals per strike on VEV_5100..VEV_5500 (fit over same strip, spread<=8, all timestamps days 0-2). Residual persistence is very high (about 0.88 to 0.97), consistent with using an EMA residual signal rather than one-tick residual for entries.
used_to_decide: Confirms persistence in residual process (supports EMA entry smoothing from v8). Tested hydrogel risk wind-down in v11 to reduce day-2 drawdown risk, but total PnL dropped materially; retained v8 as best variant.
